reforms.description: The Mexican government has initiated a programme to deepen the links between R&D in universities and industry, providing funding for training and research projects as well as the commercialisation of successful research outcomes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhances collaboration between academia and industry, fostering innovation and improving the commercial viability of research.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has supported 115 projects involving 71 firms and 60 universities and research centres.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the approach to R&D funding and collaboration, aiming to enhance innovation capacity across sectors.
reforms.source_quote: Efforts are under way to increase cross-sector linkages; this is being done through an industry/university joint programme (sponsored by CONACYT).

reforms.description: The Mexican government has established fiscal incentives for R&D, including tax deductions for current R&D expenditures and accelerated depreciation of technological assets, to promote innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These incentives are designed to stimulate private sector investment in R&D, thereby enhancing innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The Budget Law established a tax deduction for fixed expenditures for R&D directly tied to research and technology development programmes.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly alters the financial landscape for R&D investment in Mexico, encouraging greater private sector participation in innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The Budget Law established a tax deduction for fixed expenditures for R&D directly tied to research and technology development programmes.

reforms.description: The Mexican government is promoting mechanisms for public/private research cooperation to stimulate the development and application of technology across various sectors, including agriculture and resource-related industries.
reforms.package_name: National Science and Technology Development Initiative
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing collaboration between public research and industry is expected to drive innovation and improve productivity in key sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The government should focus on promoting mechanisms for public/private research co-operation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative aims to bridge the gap between research institutions and industry, which is crucial for enhancing innovation capacity in Mexico.
reforms.source_quote: The Mexican government should focus on promoting mechanisms for public/private research co-operation (including decentralisation of R&D institutions), with domestic as well as foreign firms.

reforms.description: The Ministry of Labour has expanded the scale of training programmes focused on micro and small enterprises, aiming to enhance skills and competencies in the workforce.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving the skills of workers in micro and small enterprises, the initiative is expected to enhance productivity and innovation in these sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The Ministry of Labour has expanded the scale of training programmes focused on micro and small enterprises.
reforms.importance_rationale: This expansion of training programmes is significant for enhancing the skills of a large segment of the workforce, which is vital for economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The Ministry of Labour has expanded the scale of training programmes focused on micro and small enterprises (CIMO), which is appropriate.

reforms.description: The current income tax system allows for deductions of up to 1% of total sales for investments in R&D, with an additional 0.5% for projects meeting specific criteria set by CONACYT, alongside provisions for accelerated depreciation of R&D-related equipment.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encourages private sector investment in R&D, fostering innovation and technological advancement.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax system provides for deductions and accelerated depreciation for R&D investments.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly enhances the financial incentives for private sector R&D investment, which is crucial for fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The current income tax system provides for deductions of up to 1 per cent of total sales for investments in R&D and an additional 0.5 per cent for projects meeting criteria set by CONACYT.

reforms.description: The 1996 Budget included tax deductions for small- and medium-sized enterprises that expand investment or create jobs, aiming to stimulate productive investment and employment growth.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encourages investment and job creation in the private sector, which can enhance productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The budget for 1996 included provisions for tax deductions.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform provides significant support to SMEs, which are crucial for job creation and economic dynamism, but does not represent a fundamental overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: Tax deductions in favour of small- and medium-size enterprises, as well as for all enterprises that expand investment or create jobs in 1996 on a wider scale than in 1995.

reforms.description: The 1996 Budget proposed simplified taxation for smaller enterprises and the establishment of a new autonomous Tax Administration Service to improve tax compliance and administration.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Streamlining tax administration can reduce compliance costs for businesses, potentially leading to increased investment and economic activity.
reforms.status_evidence: A Bill was sent to Congress to establish a Tax Administration Service.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform aims to enhance the efficiency of tax collection and compliance, which is significant for the overall economic environment but does not constitute a major systemic change.
reforms.source_quote: A Bill was sent to Congress to establish a ‘Tax Administration Service’ as an autonomous public entity to take responsibility for all matters concerning tax and customs administration.

reforms.description: The 1996 Budget reinforced public spending on economic infrastructure, projecting a 4% growth in public fixed investment in real terms after substantial reductions in 1995.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased investment in infrastructure can enhance productivity and facilitate economic growth by improving the business environment.
reforms.status_evidence: Public fixed investment was projected to grow by 4% in real terms.
reforms.importance_rationale: The increase in infrastructure investment is significant for economic recovery and growth, but it is part of a broader strategy rather than a standalone major reform.
reforms.source_quote: Reinforced public spending on economic infrastructure (with public fixed investment projected to grow by 4 per cent in real terms, after the substantial reduction in 1995).
